title: Estimate latent tyre-state and degradation uncertainty with a state-space filter
summary: Recent F1 literature models tyre degradation as a latent state rather than treating observed lap-time deltas as the state itself.
rationale: Recent F1 literature models tyre degradation as a latent state rather than treating observed lap-time deltas as the state itself. Hidden degradation level, slope, and uncertainty may capture cliff behavior better than raw rolling means.
implementation_hint: Fit a simple per-driver-stint linear Gaussian state-space model or Kalman-style filter on causal observations like LapTime_Delta, laptime_vs_field, and Cumulative_Degradation changes. Export filtered state, one-step-ahead residual, slope, and posterior variance as features for the classifier.
expected_effect: Could sharpen detection of imminent stops when the driver enters a degradation cliff that is only partially visible in raw lap features.
risk: Model misspecification is easy, especially around traffic and safety-car-like anomalies; if the filter is too noisy it just duplicates existing rolling features.
